page: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 30 physical cores and 60 vCPUs, utilizing hyperthreading with 2.0 threads per core. The dedicated CPU operates at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz and features 24 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 240.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 61473.27 and a PassMark CPU extended instructions test score of 54838.03, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, registering a read-only small block cached speed of 3416.59 GB/sec and an uncached large block read speed of 226.55 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1317.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1559.59, falling into the middle 50% tier. Large language model text generation is a notable bottleneck, with the small 135M model achieving only 60.5 tokens/sec, placing it in the bottom 25% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c2-standard-60 offers high compute density and dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The lack of local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. Given its strong multi-core execution and high memory bandwidth, this instance is configured for workloads such as database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 60 vCPUs and 240.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 30 physical cores. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61473.27 and uncached read speeds of 226.55 GB/sec. However, single-core metrics and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. This instance is designed for compute-intensive tasks such as database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized instance with 60 vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and compilation workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring sixty dedicated vCPUs and high memory bandwidth for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.

240.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth in the top 25% tier
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and static web serving
